political.s: Kellogg Company engaged in moderate federal lobbying and PAC contributions during 2019-2024. The company separated into Kellanov and WK Kellogg Co in October 2023, with both entities continuing lobbying activities focused on food industry policy and regulatory matters.

charity.s: Kellogg Company has committed to feeding 375 million people facing food insecurity by 2030 through its Better Days Promise. The company donated 570 million servings of food and engaged in global hunger relief efforts, supporting 252 million people since 2015.
charity.focus: Food security, hunger relief, childhood nutrition, farmer support

environment.s: Achieved 31.1% absolute reduction in Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions since 2015, with 40.3% of electricity from renewable sources in 2022. Company committed to science-based targets and supports 485,000 farmers in sustainable agriculture through its Origins programme.

labor.s: Kellogg faced significant labor disputes including a 2021 strike over wages and benefits, with NLRB actions finding unfair labor practices regarding lockouts and contract negotiations. The 2021 strike involved 1,400 workers and disputes over two-tier wage systems ($22-35/hour wage gap).

dei.s: Kellogg Company set goals for 50/50 gender parity and 25% racial representation in management by 2025, achieving 45.7% female managers and 22.7% underrepresented talent in US management by 2022. Established ERGs and diversity training programs as part of Better Days Promise.

animals.s: After 65 years of animal testing (1954-2019), Kellogg agreed with PETA to end testing on food products and ingredients except where legally required. Committed to cage-free eggs and controlled atmospheric stunning for poultry by 2024.

guns.s: Not applicable - Kellogg is a food and beverage manufacturing company with no involvement in firearms production or sales.

privacy.s: Kellogg maintains privacy policies for customer data collection and processing with commitments to protecting information. Policies address cookie usage, marketing preferences, and COPPA compliance, though specific data breach incidents and third-party sharing practices vary by subsidiary.

execPay.s: CEO Steve Cahillane earned 279:1 pay ratio compared to median worker pay in 2021 ($11.66M vs $41,815). Executive compensation averages $254,473-$5M depending on position, significantly outpacing worker wages amid labor disputes.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
